title: Lomaridium fragile
taxonomy: Lomaridium fragile is a genus of liverworts, a group of non-vascular plants that belong to the division Marchantiophyta. Marchantiophyta is a division within the kingdom Plantae. Lomaridium fragile is further classified within the class Marchantiopsida. Within the class Marchantiopsida, Lomaridium fragile is placed in the order Marchantiales. Lomaridium fragile is considered to be part of the phylum Marchantiophyta.
uses: Lomaridium fragile is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in its native regions. Its medicinal uses are often associated with treating various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Some reports suggest that the plant may have antimalarial and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, Lomaridium fragile is used to treat fever, inflammation, and other conditions. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications.
